slices.title: Suppress generic domain-balance nudges for Write
slices.notes: Generic domain-balance nudges should not imply a required Write note cadence; Write should rely on its capture-pipeline nudge instead.

slices.title: Make domain-balance nudge copy name Focus
slices.notes: Generic domain-balance copy should say a domain has not shown up in Focus lately, because the backing signal is weekly Today Focus allocation.

slices.title: Run and align package coverage for Focus balance nudges
slices.notes: Follow-up proof slice for the focused PatternNudgeRules XCTest expectations once the package test runner is available in the tracked checkout.

slices.title: Audit Today presentation of Focus balance nudges
slices.notes: Adjacent UI-contract slice to ensure Today presentation does not reframe Focus balance nudges as broad domain inactivity.

slices.title: Harden Today protocol carry-forward guardrails
slices.notes: Ensure Home protocol templates and protocol runs do not leak into Today as carried-forward Focus work unless represented by an active Home protocol run source.

slices.title: Live-populate Write capture while recording voice
slices.notes: Voice note capture should auto-populate speech-to-text while the person is talking instead of waiting silently until recording stops.

slices.title: Suppress invalid linked Train carry-forward rows
slices.notes: A linked Train Focus item should not appear as carried-forward Train work unless its linked session is still planned and actionable today.

slices.title: Add lightweight source-note conversion for Write notes
slices.notes: Write users should be able to turn an existing note into a source note from note detail without losing note text or performing a desktop-style filing workflow.

slices.title: Make Today weekly digest summary copy truthful
slices.notes: Today should show count-first weekly digest copy, avoid 0% for empty planning, and say Last Week only when the digest matches the immediately previous calendar week.

slices.title: Count completed Home protocol steps in weekly digest
slices.notes: Completed steps inside active Home protocol runs should count as completed weekly digest work without changing Home's ownership of protocol lifecycle.

slices.title: Expose and propagate Today Focus completion
slices.notes: Weekly digest completion should reflect Focus commitments that can be marked done directly in Today or by unambiguous linked source completion.

slices.title: Move Today Focus behavior into Continue
slices.notes: Focus must not render as a standalone Today section. Continue is the only Today surface for current, carried, and source-backed Focus work.

slices.title: Keep Focus-backed Continue rows legible at large Dynamic Type
slices.notes: Simulator visual pass showed the redundant trailing Focus pill crowds Continue rows at larger Dynamic Type. The row subtitle already carries the Focus reason.

slices.title: Add weekly digest versioning and safe regeneration for stale persisted digests
slices.notes: Weekly digest rule changes may make older persisted rows stale. Refresh only the latest stale digest from source data, preserve older history, and make digest calculation version metadata explicit.

slices.title: Add implementation-status markers to product contracts
slices.notes: Mark maintained product contracts with explicit implementation status, proof level, and missing/deferred work so product language is not mistaken for shipped behavior.

slices.title: Define Write promotion origin contract
slices.notes: Define the source/origin behavior for future Write Lab promotions before adding UI or destination-specific promotion flows.

slices.title: Promote Write notes into Today-owned work
slices.notes: Implement only Write to Today promotion: create Today-owned work from a WritingNote, preserve the note, link origin metadata for route-back, and define duplicate behavior.

slices.title: Promote Write notes into destination-owned tasks
slices.notes: Implement only Write to Home task promotion: create a destination-owned HomeTask from a WritingNote, preserve the note, link typed origin metadata, and define duplicate behavior.

slices.title: Route Home tasks back to source Write notes
slices.notes: Expose a visible route from Home tasks created from Write notes back to the source WritingNote. Do not implement protocol promotion in this slice.

slices.title: Document local data channel boundaries
slices.notes: Docs-only contract: clarify that local Owlory user data is scoped to the installed app identity, and source/build mirroring does not imply TestFlight/dev data sharing.

slices.title: Move resolved Train sessions into History
slices.notes: Fix Train screen projection so sessions completed, modified, or skipped today move out of the active Today section and appear in History immediately, without changing Today-domain summary semantics.

slices.title: Expose archive and delete from Write note detail
slices.notes: Add explicit note-management actions in the Write note detail sheet. Deletion must be confirmed; archive should be available when the stage transition supports it.

slices.title: Add proof-level ladder to handoffs
required_validations: python3 -m py_compile automation/supervisor/policy.py automation/supervisor/run_next.py automation/context/build_context.py automation/tests/test_harness.py
slices.notes: Add explicit proof-level vocabulary to the handoff model, prompts, docs, and focused automation tests before building running-app verification.

slices.title: Require richer handoff evidence
required_validations: python3 -m py_compile automation/supervisor/policy.py automation/supervisor/run_next.py automation/context/build_context.py automation/tests/test_harness.py
slices.notes: Extend handoff evidence with contract status changes, residual risks, repo cleanliness, and git mirror status so review can understand proof without rereading the whole diff.

slices.title: Promote Write notes into protocol drafts
slices.notes: Implement only Write to Home protocol draft/input promotion: preserve the WritingNote, store typed origin metadata, prevent duplicates, do not create active runs, and do not leak protocol drafts/templates into Today Continue.

slices.title: Make Write promotion state legible
slices.notes: Make Write note detail show promotion state for Today, Home task, and Home protocol destinations. Keep capture optional, avoid inbox-pressure copy, hide or disable duplicate actions clearly, and only add route-to-destination affordances where existing architecture supports them.

slices.title: Add schedule windows to Home protocol templates
slices.notes: Add Home-owned protocol schedule windows as template metadata with explicit Today, Weekend, This Week, or Custom labels. Windows may guide labels or overdue treatment only; they must not auto-complete, auto-abandon, or duplicate protocol runs.

slices.title: Add running app smoke proof runner
slices.notes: Add automation support for proving a runnable app can build, install, launch, and produce a screenshot on simulator. The runner must first check the Xcode project/scheme/app target contract and report a precise blocked result instead of claiming app proof when unavailable. The successful smoke proof is recorded in automation/handoffs/20260430T131834Z-owlory-running-app-smoke.json and was re-confirmed before and after Write flow verification on clean mirrored main.

slices.title: Verify Write promotion flows in the running app
slices.notes: Use the running-app smoke foundation to verify the Write note detail promotion/status flow for Today, Home task, and Home protocol destinations. This is a proof slice: do not change product behavior; if the app blocks the flow, stop with a blocked handoff and evidence.

slices.title: Capture screenshot proof for Write promotion flows
slices.notes: Add durable screenshot proof for the Write promotion/status surfaces after the running-app flow is verified. Keep this proof-oriented: capture evidence and update proof docs/handoffs without changing app behavior.

slices.title: Backfill legacy handoff proof fields where safe
slices.notes: Optional cleanup: classify older handoff artifacts that predate proof_level, missing_proof_levels, contract_status_changes, repo_clean_status, and git_mirror_status. Only backfill fields when evidence is explicit; otherwise preserve legacy-unknown and document why.

slices.title: Finish legacy handoff proof field backfill
slices.notes: Conservative second pass over the remaining legacy handoff records that batch-1 deferred. Backfill proof-era fields only from the original handoff and session evidence; preserve legacy-unknown semantics where repo cleanliness and mirror state were not recorded; do not upgrade proof claims based on later unrelated work.

slices.title: Verify Write to Home task source-note return flow on a real device
slices.notes: Proof-only slice: do not change app behavior. Build and install on a physical iPhone, capture Build Info (bundle id, build number, commit, configuration) as proof, exercise Write -> Turn into Task -> View source note end-to-end, and capture device screenshots or recorded artifacts under automation/proofs/<slice-id>/. Mark proof_level=device-verified only if the on-device flow completes; leave testflight-verified in missing_proof_levels unless TestFlight is actually used. If no device is available or the running-app-smoke build blocker is unresolved, write status=blocked with the honest reason rather than upgrading proof. First attempt blocked because Tools/generate-build-info.sh did not recognize the worktree checkout and stamped GitCommit=no-git, breaking source traceability; tools-generate-build-info-worktree-fix added as a precursor and that fix landed before this retry.

slices.title: Capture screenshot proof for Home protocol schedule labels
slices.notes: Proof-only slice: do not change app behavior. Build the worktree commit on simulator (or device), drive the Home tab to create protocols whose schedule status reaches each ScheduleStatus value (upcoming, active, satisfied, overdue), and capture durable screenshots under automation/proofs/home-protocol-schedule-ui-proof/. proof_level should land at screenshot-verified when the captures cover the full set; lower (running-app-smoke) is honest if only the launched-app state is provable. Do not mark device-verified unless an on-device pass is repeated; device-verified for the schedule labels can be a separate slice. Allowed paths exclude app source and test files; this slice writes proof artifacts and a small home.md proof-level update only.

slices.title: Allow reverting completed or skipped protocol steps to pending
slices.notes: Add a recovery affordance so a completed or skipped protocol step can be returned to pending without corrupting the run lifecycle. Concretely: (a) add a pure rule in ProtocolLifecycleRules (e.g. unresolveStep or setStepStatus to pending) that reverts a completed or skipped step's status to pending and clears its completedAt/resolvedAt metadata; (b) reverting a step that is already pending is a no-op; (c) if reverting the step makes the run no longer have all-resolved steps, reopen the run by clearing run.completedAt and (if applicable) run.status back to .active; (d) reverting one step must not disturb other resolved steps. HomeStore exposes a wrapper (e.g. revertStep(runID:stepID:)) that calls the rule and persists. HomeView exposes a clear UI action on completed/skipped steps such as a swipe-action or context-menu item labeled 'Mark Pending'. Update docs/product/domains/home.md Protocol Run Contract to state that step resolution is reversible and that reverting the final resolved step reopens the run by clearing completedAt; preserve the existing rules that resolving an already-resolved step is a no-op, that runs do not auto-complete or auto-abandon, and that abandoning remains an explicit user action. Add focused ProtocolLifecycleRulesTests for the revert state transitions (pending->pending no-op, completed->pending, skipped->pending, last-step-revert reopens run, mid-run revert leaves run active) and HomeStoreTests for the persisted wrapper. Out of scope: bulk revert across multiple steps, undo of an explicit run abandon, audit/history log of revert events, UI animation polish. proof_level should land at domain-tested unless screenshot/UI proof is explicitly captured.

slices.title: Archive protocol templates without deleting them
slices.notes: Add archived state to HouseholdProtocol so users can hide protocol templates without deleting them. Concretely: (a) add `isArchived: Bool` (default false) to HouseholdProtocol with a defaulted Codable init/decoder so legacy data without the field decodes as not archived; (b) HomeStore exposes archiveProtocol(id:) and unarchiveProtocol(id:) wrappers that toggle the flag and persist; (c) HomeView's active protocol list filters out archived templates by default and exposes Archive in the protocol detail/edit menu; (d) provide a path to view and restore archived protocols (an 'Archived' section that appears only when at least one archived template exists is fine; a global toggle to show archived templates is also acceptable). Lifecycle rules (record in docs/product/domains/home.md): archiving a protocol does NOT delete the template, its steps, or its schedule metadata; existing runs and run history remain intact; archived templates may NOT start NEW runs; ANY pre-existing active run continues until normal terminal state (completed or abandoned); when home-protocol-schedule-notifications lands, archived templates must not produce notification candidates. Add HomeStoreTests for the archive/unarchive cycle, legacy decode (protocols without isArchived field decode as not archived), the new-run block when archived (canStartRun or equivalent rejection), and that an existing active run on a now-archived protocol continues to terminate normally. Out of scope: bulk archive, time-based auto-archive, archive state on runs themselves, archive state on tasks (HomeTask already has its own management actions), per-protocol archive reason or note, archive of Write or Career records. proof_level should land at domain-tested unless screenshot/UI proof is explicitly captured.

slices.title: Triage which Owlory lists should support user reorder
slices.notes: Triage-only: do NOT implement drag-to-reorder anywhere. Inventory every visible list/list-like surface in Owlory across Today, Home, Write, Train, Patterns, Career, and reminder/digest views. Classify each list as one of: (a) user-ordered free list (likely candidates: Home Standalone Tasks, Home Protocols list, Protocol Steps within a template, Write notes within a stage); (b) derived/ranked (deterministic rule output, e.g. Today Continue via ContinueRankingRules + per-domain cap, Focus Three admission, Focus Suggestion fallback); (c) chronological (date/time-driven, e.g. Train sessions, ProtocolRun history, Write notes by createdDate); (d) rule-derived (digest/summary windows, e.g. weekly digest contents, Patterns nudges); (e) hybrid / needs design decision (lists where user expectation and current contract conflict). For each user-ordered candidate, specify the persistence model required (e.g. explicit `order: Int` field on the model, or array index in storage) and whether existing data has that field already. Identify lists that must NOT become manually reorderable because reorder would break ranking/chronology/derived contracts (Today Continue, weekly digest, Train timeline, Recent Runs, Pattern nudges). Decide which user-ordered lists should support drag-to-reorder in follow-up implementation slices; queue each implementation slice separately with explicit allowed paths, persistence work, and tests. Do not change list rendering, persistence schema, or domain rules in this triage slice. proof_level should land at doc-only. Out of scope: any drag-to-reorder UI, any new persistence field, any change to ranking or chronology rules.

slices.title: Triage Today Last Week Insights actionability options
slices.notes: Triage-only slice: do NOT implement UI behavior or change digest rendering. Decide whether Last Week Insights should remain a truthful count-first summary or gain explicit user actions, and define the smallest actionability model that does not overstate the digest signal. Read today.md weekly digest contract; inspect WeeklyDigestRules and WeeklyDigestCadenceRules along with the patterns-weekly-digest-versioned-stale-refresh and today-weekly-digest-truthful-summary-copy slices; classify three actionability options (1: per-item/domain routing — Focus summary opens Today/history surface, protocol steps opens Home protocol history, Write-related summary opens Write when the signal is actually Write-owned; 2: digest-level next-week actions; 3: pattern-driven prompts); decide which option is contract-aligned now under existing Today/Patterns/Home/Write ownership; recommend bias is option 1 first because option 3 risks implying the app knows more than the digest actually proves. If implementation is warranted, queue a separate implementation slice with narrow allowed paths and explicit proof level. Preserve proof_level=doc-only unless domain tests change. Out of scope: any code change to digest rendering, any new prompt rule in Patterns, any change to Today Continue admission, ML-generated suggestion content.

slices.title: Apply StatusBadge consistently across Train row surfaces
slices.notes: Small UI consistency slice. Inspect every Train row variant in TrainView (active SessionCardView at TrainView.swift:256, History rows at TrainView.swift:137, plus any planned/today rows or recurring sections that currently lack a per-item status pill). Apply the existing private StatusBadge component (TrainView.swift:442) so each Train item displays its own pill colored by TrainingStatus (planned, completed, modified, skipped). Reuse StatusBadge as-is unless visibility under Dynamic Type forces a small layout adjustment; in that case, prefer wrapping/stacking the pill rather than truncating the activity title. Do not redesign Train layout broadly, do not change TrainingStatus semantics, do not alter ordering, recurrence, or session lifecycle behavior. proof_level should land at domain-tested unless screenshot proof is explicitly captured. If StatusBadge needs to be made non-private to be reused across rows in the same file, that is acceptable; do not move it to a shared design-system module in this slice.

slices.title: Schedule local notifications from declared Home protocol windows
slices.notes: Declared-first only. Do NOT infer cadence from ProtocolRun history; that is a separate future slice. Add a pure planning layer in Core/Domain (e.g., ProtocolScheduleNotificationRules) that converts existing ProtocolScheduleRules.ScheduleStatus signals into plain ProtocolScheduleNotificationPlan values { protocolID, title, kind: windowOpening | overdue, fireDate, identifier }. The plan output must NOT contain UserNotifications types and Core/Domain must not import UserNotifications, UIKit, or SwiftUI notification APIs. Reminders/runtime owns delivery: extend ReminderSchedulingRules to merge protocol-schedule plans alongside the existing reminder plans, and let ReminderScheduler convert plans into UNNotificationRequest as it already does for the existing reminder plans. Notification kinds: window-opening when a schedule transitions upcoming -> active, and overdue after a defined grace period when status becomes .overdue. Starting a run for a protocol cancels stale notifications for that protocol's window; satisfied schedules do not notify. Deterministic identifier scheme per protocol/window/type so cancellation can find the request. Update home.md to state Home provides the schedule signal but does not own notification delivery; update reminders.md to claim notification delivery ownership for protocol schedule reminders; update docs/runtime/observability.md if reminder trace gains a new kind. Explicitly out of scope: derived cadence from run history, every-N-days/weekly schedule types, Today Continue projection for scheduled templates, remote push notifications, notification preference UI beyond authorization, TestFlight/device proof. proof_level should land at domain-tested unless notifications are actually exercised in the running app; do not claim higher (running-app-smoke, flow-verified, screenshot-verified, device-verified, testflight-verified all stay in missing_proof_levels until separately proven).

slices.title: Add stale/overdue treatment for scheduled Home protocol templates
slices.notes: Make scheduled Home protocol templates produce Home-owned stale/overdue state when their selected window (Today, Weekend, This Week, Custom) passes without an active or completed run, while preserving the existing rule that schedules do not auto-start, auto-complete, auto-abandon, or auto-admit templates into Today Continue. Concretely: (a) extend ProtocolScheduleRules with deterministic window-passed evaluation against an injected Date/Calendar that classifies a schedule as upcoming, in-window, or stale/overdue based on whether the window's end has passed and whether any run started during or after the window exists; (b) expose that state through HomeStore so HomeView can label scheduled templates accordingly without changing their lifecycle; (c) document the rule in docs/product/domains/home.md and explicitly state that this is Home schedule state, not Today admission. Starting a run during or after a stale window must satisfy or transition the stale state per a defined rule (do not silently rewrite the persisted window). Existing active runs must not be cleared, recreated, or auto-finished. Today Continue projection must remain unchanged; this slice does not edit today.md or any Today/Continue source. Add focused ProtocolScheduleRulesTests for window-passed classification and HomeStoreTests for the exposed state. Out of scope, do not pursue here: Today projection for scheduled templates, every-N-days/weekly cadence, scheduled-by-default for Write to Protocol promotion, richer Home schedule editing UX. Surfaced after the today-continue-write-task-projection-triage clarification confirmed schedule windows are template labels rather than admission rules; the next product step is to make those labels more meaningful in Home without reopening the protocol-template-vs-active-run boundary.

slices.title: Triage Today Continue projection for Write-origin Home tasks
slices.notes: Triage-only slice: do not implement any code change. Decide whether Home-owned Standalone Tasks promoted from Write notes (created via Turn into Task) should appear in Today's Continue projection, and under what ownership/routing rules. Read the Today, Write, and Home contracts; inspect TodayContinueSourceComposer/TodayContinuationRules and the existing Continue projection rules; check whether Home tasks are intentionally Home-only or have a missing Today carry-forward path. If intentional, document the reason in docs/product/domains/today.md (or home.md/write.md as appropriate) and close the concern. If a real gap, queue a separate implementation slice with explicit allowed paths, validations, and proof level — do not implement here. proof_level should land at doc-only (decision documented) or domain-tested (existing rule clarified with focused tests run); device-verified is reserved for any follow-up implementation slice. Surfaced by write-promotion-device-verification on commit c35a1d666e76: a Standalone Task created via Turn into Task did not appear in Today's Continue.

slices.title: Make build-info stamp script recognize git worktrees
slices.notes: Tools/generate-build-info.sh:36 currently uses `[ -d "$GIT_ROOT/.git" ]`, which fails in any git worktree because .git is a file pointer (`gitdir: /path/to/main/.git/worktrees/<name>`) rather than a directory. The script then falls through to the no-git fallback (lines 47-54) and stamps GitCommit/GitBranch/GitTag/GitCommitFull as no-git, which makes BuildInfo.isReleaseable=false and breaks any device or TestFlight provenance attempt from a worktree. Replace the existence test with one that handles both shapes (e.g., `[ -e "$GIT_ROOT/.git" ]` or `git -C "$GIT_ROOT" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1`). Verify by building from a worktree checkout and confirming Info.plist GitCommit, GitCommitFull, GitBranch, and GitTag match `git rev-parse` output rather than no-git. Do not change app behavior, only the stamp script's git-detection.

slices.title: Show live-status labels on Previous Days focus and carry-forward rows
slices.notes: Previous Days stays read-only. Add a small live-status enum (active, completed, skipped, archived, abandoned) and a resolver closure injected into PreviousDayDetailView that maps FocusItem.origin to current app state via TrainStore, WriteStore, and HomeStore. Show inline labels on focus and carry-forward rows when origin resolves; degrade silently when origin is nil or source is deleted. Support trainingSession, writingNote, homeTask, homeProtocolRun origin kinds. Do not resolve careerRecord. No DailyEntry mutation, no persistence changes, no routing to source records. Domain intentions, evening reflections, and energy/mood/sleep remain unchanged. proof_level should land at domain-tested.

slices.title: Add first-class app localization resources
required_validations: xcodebuild build -project owlory_xcode/Owlory.xcodeproj -scheme Owlory -configuration Debug -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' -derivedDataPath /tmp/owlory-localization-build CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
slices.notes: Add Apple-native Localizable.strings resources for en, ar, nl, fr, de, it, ja, ko, nb, pt, pt-BR, ru, es, sv, zh-Hans, zh-Hant, tr, uk, and vi. Use a PBXVariantGroup in the app target resources; do not copy raw lproj folders. Keep English as source and use English placeholders for non-English values in this foundation slice. Add a parity check so every locale matches English keys and wire it into architecture validation.

slices.title: Audit visible localization string extraction
slices.notes: Inventory remaining visible Swift/SwiftUI strings after the localization foundation. Classify literals as already localized by SwiftUI literal behavior, should extract now, accessibility/user-facing but needs careful wording, debug/dev-only, system/generated, or deferred. Add only obvious safe English source keys to every Localizable.strings file; keep all non-English values as English placeholders. Do not translate, rewrite product copy, or touch app source in this slice.

slices.title: Define dynamic localization formatting boundaries
slices.notes: Doc-only contract/design slice. Inventory dynamic/interpolated localization surfaces, define ownership by layer, decide when to use stringsdict/pluralization, notification localization, and model display-name adapters. Central boundary: Domain returns meaning; Application coordinates runtime-owned messages; SwiftUI presents localized display text. Do not translate placeholders or rewrite broad source strings.

slices.title: Localize Today dashboard plural counts
required_validations: xcodebuild build -quiet -project owlory_xcode/Owlory.xcodeproj -scheme Owlory -configuration Debug -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' -derivedDataPath /tmp/owlory-today-plurals-build CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
slices.notes: Implementation slice for Today dashboard counts and accessibility interpolation only. Identify Today count strings that need plural-aware formatting, add Localizable.stringsdict or the chosen Apple-native plural mechanism consistently across all approved locales, keep pure domain rules unchanged, keep non-English values as English placeholders, preserve localization parity, and do not touch digest copy, notifications, protocol schedule text, or translation quality.

slices.title: Localize weekly digest count and date labels
required_validations: xcodebuild build -quiet -project owlory_xcode/Owlory.xcodeproj -scheme Owlory -configuration Debug -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' -derivedDataPath /tmp/owlory-digest-formatting-build CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
slices.notes: Implementation slice for weekly digest count/date labels only. Move digest presentation counts, ratios, compact streaks, relative labels, and week-range labels to localized presentation formatting with explicit calendar/time-zone context. Keep WeeklyDigestRules factual, preserve cadence/stale counting/rule-version behavior, leave digest insights/highlight summaries/notification copy/protocol text/translations out of scope, and keep all locale resources in parity.

slices.title: Localize Home protocol schedule/status projection
required_validations: xcodebuild build -quiet -project owlory_xcode/Owlory.xcodeproj -scheme Owlory -configuration Debug -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' -derivedDataPath /tmp/owlory-protocol-schedule-localization-build CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
slices.notes: Implementation slice for Home protocol schedule/status display text only. Keep ProtocolScheduleRules semantic with dates, windows, presets, and statuses, move schedule/window labels into Home presentation formatting, add localization keys across all 19 locales, preserve schedule behavior and Today projection behavior, and leave notification copy, translation quality, and domain-rule rewrites out of scope.

slices.title: Localize notification titles and bodies
required_validations: xcodebuild build -quiet -project owlory_xcode/Owlory.xcodeproj -scheme Owlory -configuration Debug -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' -derivedDataPath /tmp/owlory-notification-localization-build CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
slices.notes: Implementation slice for notification titles/bodies only. Localize delivered notification copy in the reminders/runtime application path that owns scheduling, including prediction reminders, Today prompt notifications as scheduled, and protocol schedule notifications. Preserve reminder timing, suppression, dedupe, Home schedule rules, Today projection behavior, and translation placeholders. Do not move UserNotifications or copy into Core/Domain.

slices.title: Localize display-name adapters
required_validations: xcodebuild build -quiet -project owlory_xcode/Owlory.xcodeproj -scheme Owlory -configuration Debug -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' -derivedDataPath /tmp/owlory-display-name-localization-build CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
slices.notes: Implementation slice for display-name adapters only. Identify model/display labels derived from enum raw values or hardcoded adapters, move low-risk labels into UI-adjacent localized presentation formatting, keep Core/Domain semantic, do not change enum raw values or persistence, keep all 19 locale resources in parity, and leave translation quality, notification copy, Today projection behavior, and product-rule rewrites out of scope.

slices.title: Prove localization resources load in the running app
required_validations: xcodebuild build -quiet -project owlory_xcode/Owlory.xcodeproj -scheme Owlory -configuration Debug -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' -derivedDataPath /tmp/owlory-locale-smoke-build CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
slices.notes: Proof slice for representative locale launch stability only. Extend or use the running-app smoke runner to launch the simulator app with en, es, fr, ar, and zh-Hans locale arguments, verify each locale's packaged resources are present in the built app bundle, capture temporary screenshots, and report running-app-smoke proof without claiming translation quality, layout correctness, screenshot-verified proof, device proof, or TestFlight proof.

slices.title: Preserve localization locale screenshot proof
slices.notes: Proof-preservation slice only. Promote the en, es, fr, ar, and zh-Hans launch screenshots captured by app-localization-running-locale-smoke into repo-managed proof artifacts with a README/manifest and SHA-256 hashes. Claim screenshot-verified only for representative locale launch surfaces. Do not claim translation quality, full layout correctness, device proof, TestFlight proof, or change product code unless a screenshot reveals a concrete launch blocker.

slices.title: Define localization translation quality workflow
slices.notes: Planning-only slice. Define the translation review workflow, locale/native-review expectations, placeholder classification, and acceptance criteria for future translation replacement slices. Do not replace translations, fix layout, add device/TestFlight proof, change product code, or add new localization plumbing.

slices.title: Export localization translation review packet
slices.notes: Export/review workflow slice only. Generate reviewer-friendly CSV and JSON from current Localizable.strings and Localizable.stringsdict resources, including English source values, plural resources, comments when present, and per-locale status labels. Mark current non-English values as english-placeholder unless repo evidence proves otherwise. Do not replace translations, claim native review, change app resources, add locale smoke/screenshot/device/TestFlight proof, or change product code.

slices.title: Add PR and UI testing hygiene workflows
slices.notes: Harness/docs slice only. Promote Gymphant-derived PR hygiene and UI testing hygiene into Owlory's maintained workflows: proof-level claims, clean PR/handoff expectations, isolated DerivedData, deterministic UI data, screenshot/proof artifact boundaries, and failure classification. Do not add product code, create a fake UI test target, or claim new running-app/UI proof.

slices.title: Add deterministic UI seed path and first XCUITest smoke
slices.notes: Small UI harness implementation slice. Add a deterministic empty-state launch seed, one maintained XCUITest target with one Today launch-surface smoke test, isolated DerivedData validation, and docs/tooling updates. Do not broaden into a UI regression suite, product redesign, screenshot-proof preservation, or unrelated app behavior changes.

slices.title: Add deterministic Today Continue UI fixture
slices.notes: Small UI fixture follow-up. Add one deterministic seeded Continue item and one XCUITest assertion proving Today Continue can render that item. Keep product behavior unchanged, avoid screenshots/proof preservation, do not broaden into a UI regression suite, and do not touch domain policy unless a concrete harness blocker is found.

slices.title: Add deterministic Home task Continue UI fixture
slices.notes: Small UI fixture follow-up. Add one deterministic active Home task source fixture and one XCUITest assertion proving Today Continue can render source-derived Home work. Keep product behavior unchanged, do not prove routing or screenshots, do not broaden into a UI regression suite, and do not touch Continue or Home domain policy unless a concrete harness blocker is found.

slices.title: Add deterministic Continue row Done action UI smoke
slices.notes: Small interaction smoke slice. Seed one current Focus-backed Continue row, reveal the existing leading swipe action, tap Done, and assert the row leaves Today Continue through visible UI. Do not add product behavior, screenshots, broad routing proof, or a Continue source matrix. Add only stable accessibility identifiers needed for this focused action proof.

slices.title: Add deterministic Continue Home task routing UI smoke
slices.notes: Small routing smoke slice. Seed one active Home-task-backed Continue row, tap it from Today Continue, and assert Home opens with the seeded task visible through a stable Home task accessibility identifier. Do not add product behavior, screenshots, broad routing matrix, device/TestFlight proof, or behavior changes.

slices.title: Add deterministic active Home protocol routing UI smoke
slices.notes: Small routing smoke slice. Seed one active Home protocol run, verify it appears in Today Continue, tap it, and assert Home presents the active run sheet. Do not add product behavior, screenshots, broad routing matrix, device/TestFlight proof, or protocol lifecycle changes.

slices.title: Triage Today Continue source UI smoke coverage
slices.notes: Done. The Today source coverage table classifies the six real composer steps and identifies three missing source-visibility smoke cases: due-today Training, carried-forward Focus, and in-progress Writing. No UI tests or product behavior were added.

slices.title: Add deterministic Continue source UI smoke batch
slices.notes: Implementation slice after source triage. Add deterministic XCUITest smoke for the three missing source-visibility cases selected by triage: due-today Training, carried-forward Focus, and in-progress Writing. Prove source visibility only; do not add routing tests, screenshots, device/TestFlight proof, or product behavior changes.

slices.title: Triage Today Continue routing matrix
slices.notes: Doc-only triage slice. Define expected route behavior for each Today Continue source before adding more routing UI tests. Do not infer routes from wishful product copy; ground the matrix in current code and docs.

slices.title: Add deterministic Continue routing UI smoke batch
slices.notes: Implementation slice after the routing matrix triage. Per the matrix in docs/product/domains/today.md Continue UI Routing Coverage section, add deterministic XCUITest routing smoke for the two highest-value missing source routes: (1) inProgressWriting / .writingNote -> Write tab + auto-presented note detail sheet via WriteView.presentHighlightedNoteIfNeeded; (2) dueTodayTraining / .trainingSession -> Train tab + scroll-to-highlight via TrainView.highlightedSessionID. Do not add focus / carriedFocus / homeTask / homeProtocolRun routing tests; the first three are deferred per the matrix and the last is already covered. Reuse the existing seedInProgressWritingContinueItem and seedDueTodayTrainingContinueItem fixtures from the source smoke batch; no new seed argument is required. Each test should tap the seeded Continue row and assert the destination accessibility identifier (write.note.detail.<UUID> for the note-detail sheet route; the Train detail/highlight identifier should be added if not already present in TrainView). No screenshots, device/TestFlight proof, or product behavior changes unless a concrete routing bug blocks the proof. Keep make ui-smoke stable; the maintained suite should grow from nine tests to eleven.

slices.title: Close remaining Continue routing/action gaps
slices.notes: Close the routing/action gaps the routing matrix triage deferred: (1) .focusItem behavior beyond the Done action — decide whether Defer and Drop swipe actions or focus-to-linked-source tap routing warrant XCUITest coverage now; (2) .carriedFocusItem — confirm whether tapping a carried row routes by origin/linkedRecordID, stays Today-owned, or is action-only, and either add a deterministic XCUITest or explicitly classify it as not-applicable for routing with a documented reason. Update the today.md Continue UI Routing Coverage matrix so every source row reads either `covered` or `N/A by contract` with a one-line reason. No new product behavior; no carry-forward aging reset; no screenshot/device/TestFlight claim. Reuse existing seed fixtures where possible; add new launch arguments only if a behavior truly requires distinct deterministic setup. proof_level should land at running-app-smoke if new tests ship, doc-only if the outcome is a classification update.

slices.title: Preserve repo-managed screenshot proof for the maintained UI smoke suite
slices.notes: Convert the existing maintained XCUITest smoke coverage into repo-managed screenshot evidence under automation/proofs/owlory-ui-smoke-proof/. Capture screenshots from a clean commit for each maintained smoke path: Today launch surface, .focusItem source visibility, .focusItem Done action result, .homeTask source visibility + Home routing, .homeProtocolRun source visibility + active-run-sheet routing, .trainingSession source visibility + Train routing, .writingNote source visibility + Write detail-sheet routing, .carriedFocusItem source visibility, plus any routing/action artifacts added by the deferred-coverage slice. Mechanism: extend the XCUITest harness with optional XCTAttachment screenshot capture controlled by a launch flag (e.g. --owlory-ui-screenshot-capture) so attachments can be exported via xcresulttool, OR add a standalone capture script under automation/smoke/ that drives the simulator and writes PNGs directly. Each screenshot needs a stable name (01-..., 02-...), a README explaining what it proves and what it does not prove, and a manifest.json with sha256 hashes for integrity. No device or TestFlight claim. proof_level should land at screenshot-verified for the captured surfaces; do not claim broader UI regression coverage.

slices.title: Verify selected Continue smoke paths on a physical device
slices.notes: Run the maintained Continue smoke paths on a physical iPhone from a traceable build. Build Info must show the real commit/branch/build date/configuration; install must come from a clean working tree at a committed SHA (worktree-aware stamping is already shipped via Tools/generate-build-info.sh). Exercise selected Continue paths manually on device: Today launch, at least one source-visibility row per source kind, the Done action on a Focus row, and the Write detail-sheet auto-present route. Capture device screenshots or short screen recordings as durable artifacts under automation/proofs/owlory-ui-device-proof/ with a README, manifest, and the device's Build Info screenshot as the first artifact. proof_level should land at device-verified for the exercised paths only; testflight-verified remains in missing_proof_levels. Out of scope: extending automated XCUITest to device target, broad regression on device, anything that requires a TestFlight build.

slices.title: Verify selected Continue flows from a TestFlight build
slices.notes: Verify selected Continue flows from a TestFlight build. Constraint: the existing debug-only --owlory-ui-seed-* paths in OwloryUITestSupport are gated by #if DEBUG and are not present in a TestFlight (Release) build, so they cannot be assumed. This slice must first pick one path: (a) verify using real user data captured in a documented dataset; (b) add an explicitly TestFlight-safe fixture/demo-mode toggle compiled into Release builds and document its scope and access path (must be intentional, narrow, and not user-data-destructive); or (c) limit TestFlight proof to launch + Build Info verification only and explicitly classify Continue rendering as device-verified-only. Record the chosen approach in docs/workflows/release.md or ui-testing-hygiene.md before capturing artifacts. Preserve TestFlight build metadata, Build Info screenshot, and selected flow artifacts under automation/proofs/owlory-ui-testflight-proof/ with a README and manifest. proof_level should land at testflight-verified only for the verified paths; everything else stays at device-verified or below. Out of scope: device-only flows, app-wide regression on TestFlight, any change to release process beyond what the chosen approach requires.

slices.title: Define what full Owlory UI regression coverage means
slices.notes: Doc-only planning slice. Produce docs/workflows/ui-regression-plan.md that defines five lanes with explicit boundaries: smoke suite (fast, maintained, deterministic, runs locally), regression suite (broader, batched, isolated simulator state, may take longer), screenshot proof (visual evidence, distinct from XCUITest), device proof (release confidence, physical-device behavior), TestFlight proof (release-channel verification). For each lane: trigger, target, scope, what it does prove, what it does not prove, gating commands, and artifact location. Do not implement any new tests, do not extend the smoke suite, do not change product behavior. Update ui-testing-hygiene.md to cross-reference the plan. proof_level: doc-only.

slices.title: First UI regression batch: Today Continue
slices.notes: Promote Today Continue from smoke coverage to the first regression batch as defined by the regression suite plan. Scope: all six Continue sources (visibility), the routing/action paths covered or classified by the deferred-coverage slice, and selected Continue Actions (Done plus whichever Defer/Drop or Add-to-Focus paths the deferred-coverage slice classified as covered). Build the regression batch as a separate XCUITest target or class (e.g., OwloryUIRegressionTests/TodayContinueRegression) so it does not run on every `make ui-smoke` invocation; add `make ui-regression` to invoke it. Batched simulator execution is allowed when isolation is needed. Each test must reset state via existing seed paths or new deterministic seeds; do not rely on prior test residue. Classify any failure per docs/workflows/ui-testing-hygiene.md Failure Classification table. proof_level should land at running-app-smoke for the first batch; screenshot/device/TestFlight proof for regression scope is a separate slice.

slices.title: Audit Release/TestFlight build-info provenance stamping
slices.notes: Provenance-gate-failure-driven audit. owlory-ui-test-testflight-proof halted at the Build Info gate because the installed TestFlight build (commit a2229f97ae250c2bd48971cf5fa409fd21e37912, build 20260417081911) did not match committed source (commit's pbxproj CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION = 20260417081904). The slice's captured screenshot does not show GitStatus directly. First action of this audit: have the operator scroll the on-device Build Info sheet of the same TestFlight install and confirm GitStatus. Then identify the source of the build-number drift: (a) archive-time pbxproj mutation that was never committed, (b) post-archive build-number rewrite by App Store Connect or release automation, or (c) stamp script reading a mutated pbxproj while reporting clean. Depending on diagnosis, fix the stamp script (Tools/generate-build-info.sh and/or BuildInfo.swift), the archive workflow (docs/workflows/release.md), or both, so that any future TestFlight build whose Build Info reports a clean GitStatus also has a build number matching committed source. Add a release-runbook step that verifies the gate before TestFlight upload using Tools/verify-build-provenance.sh --require-clean against the archived commit and build number. Preserve audit findings under automation/proofs/owlory-release-provenance-stamp-audit/ with a README explaining what was confirmed, what changed, and how to reproduce the verification. proof_level should land at doc-only if the audit is documentation-only, domain-tested if BuildInfo or the script change ships with focused tests. Out of scope: extending UI testing scope, adding TestFlight fixture modes, or re-attempting testflight-verified — that re-attempt is a separate slice that depends_on this audit landing.

slices.title: Add committed-build-number gate to release-check + provenance verifier
slices.notes: Implement the release-workflow gate fix identified by the stamp audit. Diagnosis recorded in automation/proofs/owlory-release-provenance-stamp-audit/: the release workflow's current clean-tree gate is necessary but insufficient. An archive was uploaded to TestFlight whose CFBundleVersion (20260417081911) was never committed to any pbxproj state on any branch or in the reflog, while the stamp script reported GitStatus=clean (visible Commit/Full commit/Tag fields lacked -dirty suffixes). Required change: extend Tools/verify-build-provenance.sh to assert that the current pbxproj CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION matches a committed pbxproj state at HEAD (i.e., `git show HEAD:owlory_xcode/Owlory.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj` contains the same CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION value). The check should fail loudly with a message instructing the operator to commit the build-number bump before re-running the verifier. When invoked with --require-clean (as `make release-check` does), the new assertion must also fire. Update docs/workflows/release.md to record the strengthened gate as a numbered step before `Archive in Xcode` (the existing step 10), and to call out the failure mode this catches (uncommitted set-build-number.sh bumps that produce TestFlight builds whose build number is not reproducible from committed source). Add a focused test under automation/tests/ that simulates the failure mode (dirty pbxproj with a bumped value vs clean HEAD) and asserts the verifier exits non-zero. Out of scope: surfacing GitStatus directly in BuildInfoView (the UI gap noted in the audit is a separate product question), extending the audit to App Store Connect API checks, fixing prior unreproducible TestFlight builds retroactively. proof_level should land at domain-tested for the new verifier test path; the workflow doc change is doc-only addressing release.md.

slices.title: Display GitStatus in Build Info
slices.notes: Immediate small runtime diagnostics improvement. Surface the stamped GitStatus field directly in Build Info so future device/TestFlight provenance gates do not have to infer clean/dirty state from commit suffixes or releaseability banners. Do not change stamping behavior, release provenance rules, or TestFlight proof claims. Build Info should show GitStatus directly as clean, dirty, or unknown/unavailable while preserving the existing Not releaseable banner. proof_level target: build-tested.

slices.title: Refresh default Xcode simulator destination to iOS 26.5
slices.notes: Harness-only follow-up after installing the Xcode 26.5-compatible iOS simulator runtime. The repo default still points at iPhone 16 / iOS 26.3.1, which is no longer available on this machine and causes make test-domain DOMAIN=runtime to fail unless OWLORY_XCODE_DESTINATION is remembered out of band. Refresh the default simulator destination used by Tools/validate.sh, Makefile UI lanes, running_app_smoke.py, and validation docs to a standard available iOS 26.5 simulator such as iPhone 17 / OS=26.5. Do not touch product code or proof artifacts. Keep OWLORY_XCODE_DESTINATION override support. proof_level target: tooling-tested.

slices.title: Retry TestFlight proof with clean provenance
slices.notes: Blocked entry condition: a new TestFlight build exists from a clean, committed source state, and Build Info shows Release configuration, matching commit/build provenance, and clean GitStatus. Retry TestFlight proof only after that gate can be evaluated. Build Info gate comes first; reject the run if commit/build provenance does not match committed source, or if GitStatus is dirty or unavailable. Do not use debug seed flags, do not synthesize data, and capture only natural-data Continue paths exposed by the TestFlight install. If the gate fails, block the slice and do not capture Continue surfaces. proof_level target: testflight-verified for only the exercised TestFlight paths.

slices.title: Capture TestFlight Continue proof artifacts
slices.notes: Blocked entry condition: TestFlight Build Info provenance gate passed for the installed build. After TestFlight provenance passes, preserve screenshots and manifest evidence for the launch surface and any natural-data Continue routes actually exercised. Claim only captured surfaces. Preserve README.md and manifest.json with hashes. Do not claim debug-seeded parity, device/debug proof from TestFlight artifacts, or all Continue sources unless the TestFlight install naturally exposes them and they are exercised. proof_level target: testflight-verified for captured surfaces only.

slices.title: Ingest first reviewed locale translations
required_validations: xcodebuild build -quiet -project owlory_xcode/Owlory.xcodeproj -scheme Owlory -configuration Debug -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' -derivedDataPath /tmp/owlory-reviewed-locale-build CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
slices.notes: Blocked entry condition: reviewed translation values exist with reviewer basis/status metadata. Ingest one reviewed locale or small reviewed subset from the translation review packet, replacing only values with reviewer/status metadata. Do not bulk-replace all locales. Keep unreviewed locales as english-placeholder. Preserve key and plural parity. Do not claim native review for unreviewed locales, add new localization plumbing, or claim device/TestFlight proof unless separately captured. proof_level target: build-tested.

slices.title: Expand Owlory UI regression coverage to the next selected surface
slices.notes: Blocked entry condition: a concrete next UI regression surface is selected and scoped. Expand beyond Today Continue only after a concrete target surface is chosen, such as Write, Home protocols, Train, Patterns, or localization layout. Do not create a broad app-wide suite in one pass, expand without a named surface and coverage goal, or blur proof lanes. Use deterministic seed data, keep simulator state isolated, and classify failures instead of broadening scope. proof_level target: running-app-smoke.
